allies.rationale_text: Critical-mass gives network-effects its threshold logic by marking the point where participation becomes self-sustaining.
allies.source_quote: The core definition and purpose of Critical Mass is the idea that a system, concept, or product must reach a certain threshold or minimum density before it can become viable or self-sustaining.

allies.rationale_text: Feedback-loops explain how participation can reinforce future participation once the system starts communicating success back into itself.
allies.source_quote: The process itself creates feedback loops that communicate success or failure and allow for continuous improvement.

allies.rationale_text: Systems-thinking helps network-effects focus on interrelationships and adoption structure rather than isolated user counts.
allies.source_quote: Network Effects are fundamentally based on understanding how parts in a system interact, leading to smarter planning and better decisions.

allies.rationale_text: Pareto-principle supports network-effects by concentrating effort on the small share of users or features most likely to push the system past the threshold.

antagonists.rationale_text: Cognitive-load-theory conflicts with network-effects when a complex product explanation overloads the audience before adoption can spread.
antagonists.source_quote: When explaining a complex network or product, overloading the audience's working memory with too many features or abstract details hinders the assimilation of new information.

antagonists.rationale_text: Confirmation-bias distorts network-effects by mistaking local traction for true critical mass and ignoring disconfirming evidence about viability.
antagonists.source_quote: Mistaking Local Success for Critical Mass

structured_tensions.tension_text: Network-effects conflicts with base-rates when projected network growth extrapolates without accounting for historical failure rates of platforms that never reached critical mass.
structured_tensions.source_quote: network-effects conflicts with base-rates (base rates) when projected network growth extrapolates without accounting for historical failure rates of platforms that never reached critical mass.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Network-effects conflicts with margin-of-safety when confidence in network-effect moats reduces investment in redundancy and fallback strategies.
structured_tensions.source_quote: network-effects conflicts with margin-of-safety (margin of safety) when confidence in network-effect moats reduces investment in redundancy and fallback strategies.

curation_notes.summary: The retained relation surface stays conservative around thresholds, feedback, system structure, and anti-false-critical-mass discipline.
donor_drops: Did not preserve broader social-influence expansion because the current vocabulary already supports a tighter threshold-and-feedback surface.
open_questions: The source is stronger on allies and anti-patterns than on explicit current-vocabulary tension phrasing, so structured tensions stay omitted.
